title: Allow for radio buttons in forms and ways to limit drop down boxes
excerpt: I’m migrating over from another project management system and one thing that was useful on forms I feel like could be useful in monday.com (if it’s not already there and I just don’t know yet).  Two ideas here, on the fo…

posts.author: jfg863 (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m migrating over from another project management system and one thing that was useful on forms I feel like could be useful in monday.com (if it’s not already there and I just don’t know yet). Two ideas here, on the form being able to limit how many selections a user can make on the drop down AND potentially adding the option for a radio button. If only one selection is required then why click a drop down and waste time clicking twice (once for opening the drop down and another for clicking the selection) when you could have a radio button with all the options displayed and the user clicks the one they need to click and there is only 1 click needed. With a large form this saves alot of time.

posts.author: PM_Karen (Karen H)
posts.cookedHtml: My team was asking for the radio button feature in forms, as well. It will help save some steps, especially for those working on mobile devices!

posts.author: efarmer (Ethan)
posts.cookedHtml: My team would really benefit from radio buttons instead of just dropdowns as well.
